articles.title: Rivian will deliver the first R2 SUVs on June 9
articles.description: The company has finally set a date for the first customer deliveries of what CEO RJ Scaringe has said is "maybe the most important thing we’ve launched to date."

articles.title: Triomics nabs $22M to bring oncology-specific AI to cancer centers
articles.description: The Series B round was led by Battery Ventures.

articles.title: In more good news for Amazon, Snowflake signs $6B deal with AWS for AI CPU chips
articles.description: Snowflake has signed a new, enormous five-year deal with Amazon to secure chips for AI usage. Nvidia is once again being put on notice.

articles.title: Payroll startup Remote says it grew revenue 50% per employee without adding headcount
articles.description: Payroll service provider Remote recently surpassed $300 million in annual recurring revenue (ARR) and became cash-flow positive, thanks to a 50% increase in revenue per employee resulting from AI adoption.

articles.title: UK Visa Portal exposed thousands of applicants’ passports and selfies — then called the lawyers on us
articles.description: The third-party website exposed passports, selfies, and the location data of applicants who submitted their documents as part of the U.K. visa application process. Instead of fixing the issue, the website sent attorneys.

articles.title: Meta launches Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp subscriptions, with more to come, including AI plans
articles.description: Meta is rolling out paid subscription plans for Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp worldwide, while also testing new AI, creator, and business-focused offerings under its broader “Meta One” subscription brand.

articles.title: With a new $100M raise, Princeton’s Thea Energy is now a top-funded fusion startup
articles.description: Thea Energy's pixel-inspired magnets could give its power plant plans a boost. The fusion startup hopes to get a commercial reactor working by 2034.

articles.title: CrowdStrike and Google take down botnet used by hackers to target open source software developers
articles.description: Cybercriminals used the Glassworm botnet to infect open source software projects with malware, and in turn hack the developers and companies that use that software.

articles.title: AI coding startup Cognition raises $1B at $25B pre-money valuation
articles.description: As Cognition reaches $492 million in annualized revenue run rate, it more than doubled its valuation in eight months, it says.

articles.title: FAA orders SpaceX to investigate Starship V3 booster failure
articles.description: Starship is grounded until SpaceX finds out why the first V3 booster failed during its first test flight.

articles.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close today: Nominate a founder or submit your startup
articles.description: Today is the final day to apply or nominate a startup for Startup Battlefield 200. Once the clock strikes 11:59 p.m. PT, the window closes on your chance to compete for $100,000 in equity-free funding, gain global visibility, connect directly with investors, and launch on the TechCrunch Disrupt stage.

articles.title: ElevenLabs’ new music-generation model can switch genres mid-track
articles.description: ElevenLabs' new model will let users regenerate a section of a song without affecting the rest of the track.

articles.title: Spotify now lets you ‘clip’ moments from your favorite podcast
articles.description: With the new scissors icon, you can clip favorite moments from podcasts and share them with your audience.

articles.title: SOND, a sleep tech startup from Bose’s former head of sleep, exits stealth with $7M
articles.description: SOND introduced its debut product: Dreambuds, a closed-loop, in-ear system that captures 12 physiological signals from the wearer, then acts on them in real time to help consumers get better sleep.

articles.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket savings end in 3 days
articles.description: There are only 3 days left to save up to $410 on your ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Early Bird pricing ends May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT, and once the deadline passes, ticket prices increase. If you plan to attend one of the most influential gatherings in tech this year, now is the time to lock in your pass before rates go up again.

articles.title: Google just broke SEO. Here’s what replaces it.
articles.description: Google I/O made it official: AI-generated answers are now front and center in search, and most brands have almost no visibility into how AI is describing them to their customers. For anyone who has spent years building a strategy around 10 blue links, the rules just changed in a pretty significant way.  On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Rebecca […]

articles.title: China is increasingly keeping its best AI talent to itself
articles.description: China's AI boom is producing world-class talent, and Beijing is increasingly reluctant to let them go elsewhere.

articles.title: ClickHouse triples annualized revenue to $250M, charting a path toward an IPO
articles.description: The database provider is eyeing a public debut within the next few years.

articles.title: YouTube will now automatically label AI videos
articles.description: YouTube will now automatically label videos that use significant photorealistic AI, instead of relying solely on creators to disclose AI-generated content themselves. It's also making AI labels more prominent.

articles.title: Tech CEOs are apparently suffering from AI psychosis
articles.description: "CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis," Box CEO Aaron Levie opines. Maybe that explains the almost religious belief in AI productivity gains.

articles.title: California defeats Tesla's attempt to throw out racial discrimination lawsuit
articles.description: California civil rights agency hails win over Tesla, anticipates trial in July.

articles.title: Websites have a new way to spy on visitors: analyzing their SSD activity
articles.description: Telltale SSD activity can be measured in the browser using simple JavaScript.

articles.title: Mystery GPS jammer in Iran becomes test for NASA satellites’ capabilities
articles.description: NASA science satellites show dual use in locating sources of GPS interference.

articles.title: Mina the Hollower is the best old-school action adventure I've played in a while
articles.description: Smooth movement, compelling combat, and tons of secrets make for an innovative throwback.

articles.title: Nvidia bets $150B on Taiwan as Trump's plan to make US an AI hub backfires
articles.description: Nvidia will invest $150 billion a year to make Taiwan an AI “epicenter.”

articles.title: Roku OS’s home screen now features a large, permanent ad
articles.description: “I don't want recommendations! I know what I want to watch."

articles.title: Valve's Steam Deck is back in stock after months, but you won't like it
articles.description: Four-year-old handheld is saddled with an unfortunately modern price tag.

articles.title: Trump admin to block Ebola-exposed Americans from US, move them to Kenya
articles.description: Trump official asked CDC staff to volunteer to screen travelers at airports.

articles.title: "Little red dot" in early Universe is a naked supermassive black hole
articles.description: The black hole accounts for over two-thirds the mass of the object it inhabits.

articles.title: US Space Force confirms SpaceX will build sensor-to-shooter targeting network
articles.description: "We aren’t trading speed for scale; we are demanding both," says the military's program manager.

articles.title: Disclosure Day final trailer features Spielberg himself
articles.description: Director describes how his views on existence of aliens have changed, interspersed with footage from film.

articles.title: YouTube to begin automatically labeling AI videos
articles.description: AI videos that are animated, unrealistic, or only have a little AI may still hide their origins, though.

articles.title: Smart light company Govee apologizes for “white supremacy” marketing imagery
articles.description: PR exec says Govee "did not meet the standard required."

articles.title: Nvidia kills Windows XP-era Control Panel "after 20 years of dedicated service"
articles.description: Nvidia says the Control Panel's features have been migrated to the Nvidia app.

articles.title: Volvo gets US government approval to bypass Chinese connected-car ban
articles.description: The ban for model year 2027 onward began under Biden and has been enacted by Trump.

articles.title: Motorola's 2026 Razrs are almost worth buying just for their stunning looks… almost
articles.description: Pretty little phones with pretty big price tags.

articles.title: US law enforcement warns of "anti-tech extremism" as AI hatred grows
articles.description: The feds are raising the alarm about a new category of threat.

articles.title: Is Peter Thiel the target of Pope Leo's Gandalf quote? An investigation.
articles.description: Parsing a papal proclamation.

articles.title: Musk says US military suicide drones used Starlink in violation of SpaceX rules
articles.description: Musk says drones used Starlink instead of Starshield, blames military contractor.

articles.title: NASA takes steps toward building Moon Base, including discussing a "perimeter"
articles.description: "We also obviously want to be very mindful of the Outer Space Treaty."

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI与医疗创新**：Triomics获2200万美元融资，用于将肿瘤学专用AI引入癌症中心；AI编程公司Cognition以250亿美元估值融资10亿美元；ElevenLabs推出可中途切换风格的音乐生成模型。  
2. **云服务与芯片合作**：Snowflake与亚马逊AWS签署60亿美元AI芯片协议，进一步巩固亚马逊在云市场的优势。  
3. **网络安全与隐私漏洞**：英国签证门户网站泄露数千申请者的护照和自拍照片；CrowdStrike与谷歌联合打击针对开源开发者的僵尸网络。  
4. **电动汽车与航天进展**：Rivian将于6月9日交付首批R2 SUV；FAA要求SpaceX调查Starship V3助推器故障。  
5. **企业增长与平台更新**：薪酬初创公司Remote实现人均营收增长50%且未增加员工；Meta推出Instagram、Facebook和WhatsApp订阅服务，未来将包含AI计划；Spotify新增播客片段剪辑功能。  

**今日最值得关注的发展**：AI领域持续吸引巨额资本，Cognition以250亿美元估值融资10亿美元，同时Triomics和ElevenLabs等垂直AI应用公司也获得显著投资，显示AI在编程、医疗和创意领域的商业化加速。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **AI基准测试与模型性能**：IBM与Artificial Analysis联合发布的ITBench-AA基准测试显示，前沿模型在企业IT代理任务中的得分低于50%，表明AI在复杂企业场景中的表现仍有限。
2. **开源与本地化部署进展**：Reachy Mini机器人实现完全本地化运行；Hugging Face推出万亿参数模型的Delta Weight同步功能（TRL），支持高效参数更新；Warp公司押注基于GPT-5.5的开源开发。
3. **企业级AI应用与代理工具**：Cisco与OpenAI合作推出Codex，重新定义企业工程；OpenAI发布自优化税务代理、Virgin Atlantic使用Codex加速航运，以及AdventHealth推进全人医疗AI应用。
4. **AI战略与采购趋势**：文章指出“专业化优于规模”是多数AI采购决策忽视的关键变量，强调领域定制化比单纯扩大模型参数更有效。
5. **2026年AI安全与内容合作**：OpenAI公布2026年选举信息保障措施，并与巴西Grupo Folha、Grupo UOL达成战略内容合作；Gartner将OpenAI评为企业编码代理领域的领导者。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：前沿模型在企业IT任务基准测试中得分不足50%，凸显了当前AI在复杂企业场景中的能力瓶颈，而专业化与本地化部署正成为突破方向。
